――それでシャオレイちゃんの先生になったんですね、司令官。: So that's how you became Xiaolei's teacher, Commander.
仕方ないだろ？ あんな怪力につかまれたら逃げられないし、<br>それに前線基地の:
What was I supposed to do? Once she got a hold of me with that
freakish strength, I couldn't get away. And besides, she's promising as a combat asset for the Frontline Base.
戦力として扱うのはいいんですが、<br>先生としてしっかり教え導いてあげてくださ:
It's fine to treat her as a combat asset, but please make sure to
teach and guide her properly as her teacher.
そのつもりではある、が……教えることなんてあまりなさそうだぞ。<br>シャオレイ:
I intend to, but... it doesn't seem like there's much I can teach her.
She's way stronger than I am, after all.
欠けてる常識を補って、あいつが困らないようにしてやればいいだろう。<br>そうす:
I just need to fill in the gaps in her common sense and keep her out
of trouble. If I do that, she'll become a solid combat asset...
きゃあっ！？<br>司令室のドアが、吹き飛んで……っ！？: Kyaah! The command room door... it's been blown away...!
何が起きたっ！？ 厄災か、モンスターか！？<br>どこぞの諜報員の破壊活動かっ！:
What's going on? A disaster? A monster? Or is it sabotage by some
spy?
身内の犯行かーっ！<br>何をやってるんだお前はっ！！！: It was one of our own! What do you think you're doing!
そういうことじゃない！<br>どうしてドアを吹き飛ばしたんだ！？: That's not the point! Why did you blow the door away?
ノックしたら、飛んだ。わざとじゃない。: When Xiaolei knocked, it flew off. Xiaolei didn't mean to.
ただのノックで……？<br>あ、あはは……とんでもない力ですね……: Just from a knock...? Ahaha... what incredible strength...
……待てよ。わざとじゃないってことは、もしかして……。<br>シャオレイ、ちょっ:
...Hold on. If it wasn't on purpose, then maybe... Hey Xiaolei, could
you try holding this cup?
あぁ、やっぱりな……こいつ、力が強すぎて加減ができてないんだ。<br>こりゃ思っ:
Ah, just as I thought... She's too strong and can't hold back. This
might be more of a problem than I expected...
う、ううん……教えること、たくさんありそうですね？: W-well... It looks like you have plenty to teach her, huh?
さてシャオレイ。<br>まず確認しておくが……お前、自分の力が制御できてないな？:
Alright, Xiaolei. First, let me confirm something... You can't control
your own strength, can you?
先生、しつれい。そんなこと、ないもん。: Teacher, that's rude. That's not true!
うーん、自覚はない、か。<br>じゃあどうしてドアが吹き飛んだり、持ったコップが:
Hmm, so you don't realize it, huh? Then why do you think the door
flew off and the cup you were holding shattered?
家にあるものは取ってもこわれなかった。<br>こんなに、かるくもない。:
At home, things didn't break when Xiaolei picked them up. They
weren't this light either.
あー……そういや、シャオレイに聞いたな……: Ah... now that you mention it, I did hear that from Xiaolei...
……まあ、ワシが何も教えんでも、<br>シャオレイは生まれつき強かったがのう。: ...Well, even if I didn't teach her anything, Xiaolei was born strong.
シャオレイの怪力は生まれつきだったんだよな。<br>それに合わせて家のものは全て:
So Xiaolei's super strength is innate, huh. They must have replaced
everything at home with sturdy, heavy items to match it.
もしかしたら鍛えるために、どんどん重くしてたのかもしれない。<br>重りをつけて:
Maybe they gradually made them heavier to train her. Like living with
weights on.
……？<br>ものって、勝手に、重くなるんじゃ？: ...? Do things just get heavier on their own?
ならない！ それはもうトレーニングなんだよ！: No, they don't! That's called training!
というわけで、だ。<br>シャオレイの力は戦闘なら有用だが、日常生活にはちょっと:
Anyway. Your strength is useful in combat, but it's a bit excessive for
daily life.
これから問題なく生活できるように、<br>まずは力の制御を学んでもらおうと思う。:
I think you should start by learning to control your strength so you
can live without problems.
それ、はんたい。シャオレイ、強くなりたい。<br>力を弱めるひつようない。:
That's wrong. Xiaolei wants to get stronger. There's no need to
weaken it.
ところが必要あるんだよ。<br>力の制御は強くなるためにも大切なんだぞ。:
But there is a need. Controlling your strength is also important for
getting stronger.
常に全力を出してると、体力の減りも早いし、<br>フェイントなんかの搦手もやりに:
If you always go all out, you'll burn through stamina quickly, and
feints and other tricky moves become harder to do.
それに本当に強い武人ってのは、<br>剛力よりも脱力を重視するもんだ。:
Besides, truly strong martial artists value relaxation over brute
strength.
だつりょく……。<br>きいたことある、さすが先生。: Relaxation... Xiaolei has heard of that. As expected of Teacher.
聞き分けがよくて大変よろしい。<br>じゃ、早速特訓だ。: Good, you're listening well. Then, special training right away.
おひるごはん食べるの？<br>やった。ちょうど、おなかへってた。: Are we eating lunch? Yay! Xiaolei was just hungry.
違う違う違う。シャオレイにはここで食器洗いをしてもらうんだ。<br>割れやすい皿:
No, no, no. I'm going to have you wash dishes here. Washing fragile
plates without damaging them is good practice for strength control.
お皿洗い、とくい。<br>こきょうでも、お手伝いしてた。: Xiaolei good at washing dishes. Xiaolei helped at home too.
お、そりゃよかった。<br>ゆっくりでいい、丁寧にやるんだぞ。: Oh, that's good. Take your time and do it carefully.
うん。どーんとまかせて。<br>じゃあ――: Yeah. Leave it to Xiaolei. Then—
胸を張って言うな！！<br>洗い終わったんじゃなく、物理的に皿がなくなってるじゃ:
Don't puff out your chest like that! You didn't finish washing
them—they're physically gone!
くっ、仕方ない……。<br>それじゃあ、別の特訓だ！: Ugh, fine... Then we'll do different special training!
いいかシャオレイ、今からやってもらうのは、マッサージだ。<br>俺の固まった筋肉:
Listen, Xiaolei. What I want you to do now is give me a massage.
Loosen up my stiff muscles.
さすがに人は壊したりしないだろう。<br>……しないよな？ しないでくれよ？: Surely you won't break a person... You won't, right? Please don't.
まっさーじ……あんま、のこと？<br>それならとくい。: Massage... you mean that sort of thing? Then Xiaolei good at that.
本当か……？<br>じゃあ、俺はここに座ってるから。まずは肩叩きから頼む。: Really? Then I'll sit here. First, start with tapping my shoulders.
まかせて。<br>先生のかた、頑張って、たたく。: Leave it to Xiaolei. Teacher's shoulders, Xiaolei will tap hard.
頑張らなくていい、絶対に頑張らなくていい。<br>可能な限り力を抜いてやってくれ:
Don't try hard! Definitely don't try hard! Use as little strength as
possible.
開いた窓から部屋の中へ、<br>ふわふわと蝶が迷い込んできた。: A butterfly came fluttering into the room through the open window.
シャオレイの瞳がゆらゆらと飛ぶ蝶へと吸い寄せられる。<br>ゆらりゆらりと、羽ば:
Xiaolei's eyes were drawn to the fluttering butterfly. Her body
swayed gently in time with its wings.
あ……そうだった。<br>先生のかたを、とん、とん。: Ah... right. Teacher's shoulders, tap, tap.
おわあああっ！？<br>椅子が！ 椅子がぶっ壊れたっ！？: Whoaahh! The chair! The chair's totally busted!
わ……ちゃんと、見てなかった。<br>つぎ、先生のかた、たたく。:
Oh... Xiaolei wasn't watching properly.
Next, Xiaolei will tap Teacher's shoulder.
いや、もういい！ 叩かなくていいぞ！<br>むしろ外れて良かった、本当に！:
No, that's enough! You don't need to tap! Actually, I'm glad it missed,
seriously!
ま、また別の特訓にしよう。<br>次はアプローチの方向を変えて――:
Well, let's do a different kind of training.
Next, we'll change our approach—
掃除もダメ、か……: So even cleaning's no good, huh...
すぐには難しかったか。<br>戦闘力がある代償と考えれば、仕方ない部分もあるしな:
I guess it was tough right away. But if you think of it as the price for
your combat power, it's understandable.
ごめん、なさい。<br>先生、おしえてくれた、のに……。:
Xiaolei is sorry.
Teacher taught Xiaolei, but...
心構えができたってことで、今日のところは良しとしよう。<br>気長にやっていくぞ:
Let's see it as you getting into the right mindset, and call it good for
today. We'll take our time.
うぅ……つづけないと、ダメ？: *whimper*... Does Xiaolei have to keep going?
そりゃダメだ。<br>なんでもかんでも壊してたら周りの人も困るんだからな。:
Of course you can't stop.
If you go around breaking everything, you'll cause trouble for others.
司令官殿！ 司令室の修理、完了いたしました！<br>新しい皿もすぐに納品される予:
Lord Commander! Repairs to the command room are complete!
New plates will be delivered shortly!
ああ、シャオレイがぶっ飛ばしたドアの修理をしてくれたんだよ。<br>割れた皿の発:
Oh, that's the guy who fixed the door you blew off. He also ordered
new plates for the broken ones, so go thank him later, okay?
壊れたものを放っておくわけにはいかないからな。<br>修理もするし片付けもする、:
We can't just leave broken things lying around.
We get them repaired, cleaned up, and restocked.
わたしの、せい……。<br>シャオレイ、めいわく、かけてた。: It's Xiaolei's fault... Xiaolei was causing trouble.
しぇしぇ、パンパン、ダンダン……。<br>シャオレイ、がんばる。: Thank you, Panpan, Dandan... Xiaolei will do her best.
シャオレイは涙をこらえ、<br>パンパンとダンダンを優しくなでた。: Xiaolei held back her tears and gently stroked Panpan and Dandan.
まあ、そう焦る必要はないさ。その気持ちがあれば必ず改善する。<br>そいつらも支:
Well, no need to rush. With that attitude, you'll improve. And those
two are supporting you too...
ま、待てよ……！<br>そうか、これならもしかしたら――: W-wait! I see... With this, maybe—
